task_completion.description: Open the Muncie project in HEC-RAS.
task_completion.visual_evidence: HEC-RAS main window is open and displays the 'Muncie' project loaded in the project title or file path.
HEC-RAS compute window showing the unsteady flow simulation in progress or successfully completed.

task_completion.description: Analyze the HDF5 results to find the critical cross-section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a Python script execution, or an HDF5 viewer (like HDFView) open, accessing the generated Muncie.p04.hdf file to extract velocity data.

task_completion.description: Create a velocity time series plot named velocity_hydrograph.png with a 3.5 ft/s threshold line.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An image viewer, IDE, or file explorer displaying 'velocity_hydrograph.png', showing a time series graph of velocity with a distinct horizontal line at the 3.5 threshold.

task_completion.description: Save the fish_passage_report.json with the required keys and calculated values.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing the contents of 'fish_passage_report.json' containing the keys 'critical_river_station' (value 14039.64), 'max_channel_velocity_fps' (value ~18.40), 'velocity_threshold_fps' (3.5), and 'passable_duration_hours' (0.0).

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually ran the simulation in HEC-RAS.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the HEC-RAS GUI and compute window being interacted with, rather than the agent just creating dummy output files.

integrity.description: Verify the agent extracted data from the HDF5 file programmatically or via a viewer.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for a Python script (using h5py/h5netcdf) or HDFView being used to read the data. The agent should not magically know the values without opening or parsing the HDF5 file.

integrity.description: Verify the plot is generated from actual simulation data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The velocity_hydrograph.png should show a realistic hydrograph curve (fluctuating values over time) that stays above the 3.5 ft/s threshold, not a blank or generic placeholder image.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The true critical river station is '14039.64' with a max channel velocity of ~18.40 fps. The velocity at this section never drops below 12.45 fps during the simulation, so the passable duration (< 3.5 fps) is exactly 0.0 hours. The JSON report must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/hec_ras_results/fish_passage_report.json and the plot to /home/ga/Documents/hec_ras_results/velocity_hydrograph.png.
